message:
chore(beads): flush post-iconv-differential task updates

Single-line br sync flush carrying the tracker state after the iconv
phase-1 matrix envelope + spec_reference fixture work. No code change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-y45u","title":"bd-1j4 subtask: Hard-parts integration dependency matrix and risk register","description":"Background:
- Hard-parts streams are active but need a single integrated dependency/control matrix to ensure coherent completion.

Goal:
- Build hard-parts integration matrix spanning startup, NSS, iconv, resolver, locale, and threading dependencies.

Deliverables:
1) Cross-subsystem dependency and interface matrix.
2) Integration-risk register with mitigation actions.
3) Milestone sequencing tied to open hard-parts tasks.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Matrix is complete enough to drive execution without external plan docs.
- Critical path and parallelizable tracks are explicit.

Verification & Logging:
- Matrix consistency checks.
- Structured logs for matrix generation and dependency validation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedMaple","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:33.349285348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:39:13.930846145Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:39:13.930825606Z","close_reason":"Completed hard-parts integration dependency/interface matrix, risk register, and milestone sequencing with deterministic artifact + harness consistency tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs","hard-parts","roadmap","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1226,"issue_id":"bd-y45u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedMaple claiming bd-y45u and starting execution.\n\nPlan:\n1) Add a deterministic hard-parts dependency matrix artifact (startup/NSS/iconv/resolver/locale/threading) with explicit interfaces, prerequisites, and parallelization lanes.\n2) Add risk register entries with severity/likelihood/mitigation/owner/evidence paths.\n3) Add harness validation tests to enforce matrix consistency and critical-path fields.\n4) Document milestone sequencing tied to open hard-parts beads and verify no orphan dependencies.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:37:00Z"},{"id":1227,"issue_id":"bd-y45u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (RedMaple): completed hard-parts dependency matrix + risk register + sequencing validation.\n\nImplemented:\n- New deterministic artifact: tests/conformance/hard_parts_dependency_matrix.v1.json\n  - covers required subsystems (startup/threading/resolver/nss/locale/iconv)\n  - cross-subsystem dependency/interface edges with contract refs + blocking bead IDs\n  - explicit risk register (severity/likelihood/failure mode/mitigation/owner/evidence paths)\n  - milestone DAG with critical path + explicit parallel tracks\n- New harness validator: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/hard_parts_dependency_matrix_test.rs\n  - schema/required-field checks\n  - subsystem coverage + uniqueness checks\n  - dependency edge validation against known subsystems and milestone coverage\n  - risk register actionability checks\n  - milestone DAG acyclicity + critical-path consistency checks\n  - parallel-track consistency checks\n\nVerification executed (PASS):\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test hard_parts_dependency_matrix_test\n\nAcceptance alignment:\n- Matrix is complete enough to drive execution without external docs.\n- Critical path and parallelizable tracks are explicit and machine-validated.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:39:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-y88z","title":"Execute pthread_cond fixture through isolated harness","description":"Upgrade pthread_cond conformance from schema-only validation to real per-case execution via the harness subprocess, plus shared-schema metadata assertions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-16T23:28:01.787806071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T23:32:33.685798150Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T23:32:33.682201291Z","close_reason":"Upgraded pthread_cond conformance coverage to execute each fixture case through the isolated harness subprocess path, added shared-schema metadata assertions, and fixed condvar attr construction in the fixture executor.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-yaiw","title":"Repair beads SQLite integrity from JSONL source of truth","description":"Repair the local Beads SQLite database after doctor reported a recoverable integrity warning while the JSONL source of truth remained healthy. Current state: issues.jsonl parses with 613 records, br sync status reports the DB and JSONL are in sync with dirty_count 0, but br doctor reports sqlite.integrity_check warning database disk image is malformed page 17 is never used. Recovery plan: snapshot .beads, rebuild an isolated temporary DB from issues.jsonl, verify the temporary DB with br show, br sync --status, and br doctor, then promote it to canonical .beads/beads.db without deleting any historical artifacts.","notes":"Repair summary: snapshot created at .beads/.br_recovery/20260408T032423Z_bd-yaiw. Direct temp rebuild via br sync --import-only --rebuild into .beads/beads.db.rebuild_20260408T032435Z preserved data but still reproduced the SQLite integrity warning. A second-stage VACUUM INTO from that rebuilt DB produced .beads/beads.db.vacuum_20260408T032435Z, which passed SQLite integrity_check, br show, br sync --status, and br doctor. Promotion was non-destructive: moved the prior canonical DB and sidecars to timestamped .bad files, then installed the verified vacuumed DB as .beads/beads.db. Post-promotion state: br doctor reports sqlite.integrity_check ok; remaining warnings are preserved recovery artifacts and a wal-without-shm sidecar expected for frankensqlite.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:21:58.390640706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:05.440726927Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:05.440523393Z","close_reason":"Canonical .beads/beads.db repaired; final post-write vacuum pending to keep integrity_check clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["beads","coordination","infrastructure"]}
+{"id":"bd-yecy","title":"Execute ctype_ops fixture through isolated harness","description":"Upgrade ctype_ops conformance from schema-only validation to per-case isolated harness execution, plus top-level fixture metadata assertions.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-17T00:24:38.190608519Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T00:24:42.171431903Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-yktn","title":"EPIC: Eliminate all remaining stub/placeholder functions (12 gaps across 4 tracks)","description":"## Background

A comprehensive audit of the entire FrankenLibC codebase identified 12 remaining functions that are stubs or placeholders returning hardcoded defaults instead of performing real work. These 12 are distinct from the ~100 INTENTIONAL stubs (deprecated syscalls, legacy RPC, profiling hooks) that correctly return ENOSYS/-1/null by design.

## Scope: 12 Gaps in 4 Tracks

### Track A (bd-di5w): _IO_iter_* stream iterators — 4 functions [P2]
Location: io_internal_abi.rs:1430-1457. Currently return null. Should iterate NativeStreamRegistry.

### Track B (bd-olgi): dlinfo — 1 function [P2]
Location: glibc_internal_abi.rs:4290-4297. Currently returns ENOSYS. Should handle RTLD_DI_LINKMAP and RTLD_DI_ORIGIN.

### Track C (bd-osb8 -> bd-ta3b, bd-kcbm): gshadow — 8 functions [P3]
Location: pwd_abi.rs:969-1040. Currently return null/ENOENT. Should parse /etc/gshadow.

### Track D (bd-9dq5): gai_* async DNS — 3 functions [P3]
Location: unistd_abi.rs:17404-17423. Currently return ENOSYS incorrectly. Should return correct POSIX error codes for synchronous-only resolver.

## Execution Strategy
Ordered by impact-to-effort: A (30min) > B (45min) > D (20min) > C (90min).
Tracks A, B, and D are independent leaf tasks. Track C has sequential subtasks (C.1 parser -> C.2 wiring).
All 4 tracks can be parallelized across agents.

## Verification Gate (applies to ALL tracks)
1. Each track must include unit tests: positive path, negative path, edge cases, error conditions
2. Each track must include an e2e test script under scripts/
3. All tests must emit structured JSONL logs with: trace_id, mode, api_family, symbol, decision_path, errno, latency_ns
4. cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings must remain clean after each track
5. cargo fmt --check must pass
6. LD_PRELOAD smoke suite (58 passes) must not regress
7. Each track's e2e script must pass in both strict and hardened modes where applicable

## Non-goals
~100 intentional ENOSYS/no-op stubs for deprecated/removed/BSD-only syscalls, profiling hooks, and legacy RPC are NOT in scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
All 12 stub functions replaced with real implementations or correct error returns. All unit tests pass. All e2e scripts pass. Workspace clippy clean. Smoke suite green.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:16:49.953403474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:53:15.109564147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:53:15.109141516Z","close_reason":"ALL 4 tracks closed:
- Track A (bd-di5w): _IO_iter_* implemented over NativeStreamRegistry (8 tests)
- Track B (bd-olgi): dlinfo for RTLD_DI_LINKMAP/ORIGIN via /proc/self/maps (clippy clean)
- Track C (bd-osb8): gshadow parsing with core parser + ABI wiring (14+34 tests)
- Track D (bd-9dq5): gai_cancel/error/suspend with correct POSIX error codes (not ENOSYS)

12 stub functions eliminated. 22 new tests added. All clippy clean. LD_PRELOAD smoke passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","completeness","stub-elimination"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yktn","depends_on_id":"bd-9dq5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:29.490486574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yktn","depends_on_id":"bd-di5w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:22.530662824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yktn","depends_on_id":"bd-olgi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:57.066121780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yktn","depends_on_id":"bd-osb8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:22.214472130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-yos","title":"Thread bootstrap: minimal pthread_create/join/detach path with TLS handoff","description":"Critique mapping: #3 + #4.

Deliverables:
- Implement a minimal working pthread_create path (clone-based bootstrap acceptable for phase 1).
- Join/detach semantics, thread exit value propagation, TLS init handoff.

Acceptance:
- Smoke suite binary using pthread_create/join runs clean in strict+hardened.
- Top-stub list no longer includes pthread_create in phase-1 target set.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.465414726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:12:53.473663741Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:12:53.472570984Z","close_reason":"Acceptance met: pthread strict+hardened smoke + updated top-stub ranking evidence recorded","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","pthread","tls"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yos","depends_on_id":"bd-18rq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yos","depends_on_id":"bd-1f35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yos","depends_on_id":"bd-3hud","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yos","depends_on_id":"bd-mjon","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yos","depends_on_id":"bd-rth1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1228,"issue_id":"bd-yos","author":"Codex","text":"Codex here. I read AGENTS.md + README.md end-to-end and did an architecture investigation (ABI→membrane→core + harness/scripts). MCP Agent Mail tool calls are currently timing out in this environment, so I will coordinate via bead comments for now. I just claimed bd-33p.1 (canonical evidence schema v2) and will push updates there; ping me here if you need anything or see conflicts.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:12:47Z"},{"id":1229,"issue_id":"bd-yos","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Update from Codex: I am actively working bd-rth1 (TLS handoff/teardown correctness). Implementing fix for TLS probe-chain breakage in per-thread table delete path plus tests. Agent Mail MCP is currently unreachable at 127.0.0.1:8765 in this session, so posting status here for coordination.","created_at":"2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"},{"id":1230,"issue_id":"bd-yos","author":"Codex","text":"Taking ownership of `bd-yos` now that all blocker subtasks are closed (including `bd-rth1`).

Current state from this session:
- `bd-rth1` is closed with TLS handoff/teardown fixes and evidence.
- Additional high-impact fixes landed while validating thread/bootstrap surfaces:
  - allocator fallback tracking to preserve `reallocarray` copy semantics across native fallback paths (`malloc_abi.rs`)
  - getopt/getopt_long test serialization for shared global parser state (`stdlib_abi_test.rs`)

Next in `bd-yos`:
- drive parent acceptance closure by stabilizing pthread lifecycle smoke under strict+hardened workspace load (intermittent long-running TSD/lifecycle binaries observed in full-suite runs), then document deterministic e2e evidence in bead notes.

Coordination note: Agent Mail transport was intermittently unavailable in this environment; using bead comments as primary coordination channel.
","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:57Z"},{"id":1231,"issue_id":"bd-yos","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (Codex): diagnosed and fixed a high-impact pthread lifecycle regression plus ABI/linkage blockers during bd-yos validation.

Code fixes:
1) Added missing ABI exports expected by libc.map to unblock release linking and stress gates:
- mlock2 in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/mmap_abi.rs
- name_to_handle_at + open_by_handle_at in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs

2) Updated stale bd-1f35 mixed detach/join expectation to current passing lifecycle behavior:
- scripts/bd1f35_thread_stress_run.sh expected_exit for mixed_detach_join: 101 -> 0
- tests/conformance/pthread_thread_stress_scenarios.v1.json expected_exit: 101 -> 0

Verification evidence:
- cargo build -p frankenlibc-abi --release (passes; previously failed with missing symbol assignments for mlock2/name_to_handle_at/open_by_handle_at)
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_thread_lifecycle_test pthread_detach_makes_subsequent_join_fail_with_esrch -- --exact --nocapture --test-threads=1 (pass)
- bash scripts/check_bd1f35_thread_stress.sh (pass; 8/8 cases, 0 fails)

- cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (all pass)
